name: Consumer Credit of the Quad Cities
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling organization providing free budget counseling, debt analysis, and debt management plans since 1993 in the Quad Cities area.
description_long:
Consumer Credit of the Quad Cities is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization established in 1993, serving individuals struggling with debt management in the Quad Cities region. The organization operates with a mission to help people take control of their finances through education and personalized counseling. Their primary focus is on reducing financial stress by providing tools and support to those facing credit card debt, bill collector issues, and potential bankruptcy situations.

The organization offers free confidential budget counseling sessions with certified counselors who help clients assess personal finances, establish budgets, evaluate debt, and review spending plans. Beyond one-on-one counseling, they provide a Debt Management Plan (DMP) option that consolidates multiple creditor payments into a single monthly payment processed through the organization. They also offer comprehensive financial education materials covering budgeting, spending plans, smart shopping, saving strategies, and debt repayment options. Initial consultations are free and available through multiple convenient channels including online, phone, mail, fax, and email.

Consumer Credit distinguishes itself through professional credentials and community trust markers. All counselors are certified, the organization holds 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status, maintains Better Business Bureau membership, and is certified by the Partnership for Financial Education. They have operated continuously for over 30 years with stated success helping thousands of clients achieve debt repayment goals. The organization emphasizes confidentiality and flexibility in service delivery, allowing clients to choose their preferred contact method for initial consultation.

The primary limitation is geographic scope—services appear focused on the Quad Cities area (Bettendorf, Iowa location), which may restrict access for non-local clients. While they offer free counseling, the Debt Management Plan option involves paying the organization monthly, which may not suit all financial situations. The website lacks detailed information about success rates, average program outcomes, or specific creditor negotiation results, making it difficult to assess effectiveness compared to other non-profit counseling agencies.
pros: All counseling sessions are completely free and confidential
Certified counselors provide personalized budget and debt assessment
Multiple contact methods available (online, phone, mail, fax, email)
Established non-profit with 30+ years of operational history since 1993
Better Business Bureau member and Partnership for Financial Education certified
Debt Management Plan consolidates multiple creditor payments into single monthly payment
Comprehensive financial education library and materials included
cons: Geographic limitation—appears primarily focused on Quad Cities/Iowa region
Debt Management Plan involves paying the organization monthly fees (not explicitly detailed)
Website lacks specific success rates, default rates, or average outcome data
Limited information about creditor negotiation track record or average payment reductions
No detailed explanation of how DMPs compare to alternative debt solutions
best_for: Individuals in the Quad Cities area facing multiple creditor payments and seeking consolidation
People overwhelmed by debt who need structured budget counseling and financial education
Those at risk of bankruptcy seeking alternatives and professional debt management guidance
Budget-conscious consumers who qualify for free counseling services
diagnosis: Consumer Credit of the Quad Cities is ideal for Quad Cities-area residents overwhelmed by multiple debts who want free, certified professional guidance and potentially a simplified payment consolidation solution. The main caveat is geographic limitation—out-of-area clients may not have access to services, and the organization's outcomes data is not publicly detailed, making comparative evaluation difficult.
services: Debt Management Plan (DMP) setup and administration
